member.name: Mike Martin
member.synopsis: Mike Martin is the Liberal Democrat MP for Tunbridge Wells , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Woodlands Health Centre
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department has had discussions with NHS Kent and Medway Integrated Care Board on commissioning building upgrades at Woodlands Health Centre in Tunbridge Wells constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has engaged with all integrated care boards (ICBs), including the NHS Kent and Medway ICB, as part of the process for developing the future pipeline of neighbourhood health centres. The NHS Kent and Medway ICB has submitted a proposal for t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: General Practitioners: Tunbridge Wells
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what recent assessment he has made of the adequacy of access to GP services in the Tunbridge Wells constituency.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are increasing general practice (GP) capacity and funding to improve access. The Department has not held discussions with the NHS Kent and Medway Integrated Care Board. It is for the relevant commissioner to ensure that appropriate provision is in plac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Neighbourhood Health Centres: Tunbridge Wells
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment has been made of the potential merits of establishing a neighbourhood health centre in the Tunbridge Wells constituency to meet demand for GP services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Neighbourhood health centres will be seen as the place to go for most health needs in every community. Delivery will be locally led and designed to reflect the specific needs of local populations. On 15 April 2026, we published Neighbourhood Health Centre...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Women's Health Hubs: Kent
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, how many women's health hubs have been established in NHS Kent and Medway ICB; and what services they provide.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department of Health and Social Care has indicated that it will not be possible to answer this question within the usual time period. An answer is being prepared and will be provided as soon as it is available.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Recruitment

writtenQuestions.answerText: DWP does not set or enforce expectations on how quickly employers should acknowledge or respond to job applications, but it uses its relationships with employers to encourage more timely communication throughout the recruitment process. This includes prom...

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Insulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to incentivise people to insulate their homes; and if he will set out the support available to help people cover these costs.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Insulation plays an important role in making homes cheaper to heat and healthier to live in, and many homes, particularly those at risk of fuel poverty, stand to benefit from cost-effective fabric upgrades through the £15 billion Warm Homes Plan, the bigg...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Motor Vehicles: Excise Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps she is taking to integrate DVLA and DWP data systems so that disabled people who qualify for vehicle tax reductions through receipt of PIP or DLA are not required to complete a separate annual renewa
writtenQuestions.answerText: The law requires that entitlement to Personal Independence Payment (PIP) is checked when a vehicle is taxed. The Driver and Vehicle Licensing Agency (DVLA) remains committed to working with the Department for Work and Pensions to review and improve the pr...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Sexual Offences: Sentencing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what assessment he has made of the potential merits of reviewing sentencing guidelines for serious sexual offences committed by under-16s.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Sentencing guidelines are produced by the independent Sentencing Council, in fulfilment of its statutory duty to do so.Under the current guidelines, children convicted of rape and sexual assault can be given custodial sentences.The Lord Chancellor has the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice

writtenQuestions.heading: Immigration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, when her Department plans to respond to the Earned Settlement consultation that closed on 12 February 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The earned settlement model, proposed in ‘A Fairer Pathway to Settlement’, was subject to a public consultation, which opened on 20 November 2025 and closed on 12 February 2026.We received over 200,000 responses and are now in the process of carefully con...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Voice over Internet Protocol: Care Homes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the potential financial impact on residents of privately-managed leasehold retirement housing of the requirement to upgrade communal telecare
writtenQuestions.answerText: Switching a landline from the analogue Public Switched Telephone Network (PSTN) to digital Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) should not increase costs for the end customer.However, independent businesses, including privately managed retirement housing w...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Railway Stations: Tonbridge
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how much funding has been made available to South Eastern Railway to undertake outline designs to improve car park provision at Paddock Wood station.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I can confirm that there has been no funding from the Department to Southeastern to improve car park provision at Paddock Wood train station. The Rail Minister has asked the Managing Director of South Eastern Railway to contact the MP about this matter.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Defence: Reform

writtenQuestions.answerText: Since 4 July 2024, the Department has engaged with consulting firms and their subcontractors that hold contracts supporting the implementation of the Defence Reform Programme. Information on these contracts is published and available through the Contract ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Defence

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice
